channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Canceled Garfield Platformer Seemingly Surfaces Online
item.content:encoded:
The character model and a special level from a canceled Garfield platformer have been spotted circulating on social media.

There’s a lot of canceled stuff that has surfaced online recently. Just a few days ago, we saw 10 minutes of gameplay of Quake 4’s canceled ‘Awakening’ expansion surface online. We also got to see a canceled version of Metro 4, which has been unveiled through more than 120 GB of content. Now, a canceled Garfield title has appeared.

Canceled Garfield Platformer’s Character Model and Special Level Posted

A new post by EvilLeeker on X (formerly Twitter) features a character model for an “unreleased” Garfield Dream World game. The screenshot has the words ‘Burger Rider’ written, which could be the model name, along with mentions of ‘Microids’ (a French developer and publisher) and Balio Studio, the people behind Garfield Lasagna Party.

Another X user, mrevillblionair, added more to the previous leak, with their latest post seemingly containing a “Garfield Special Level”. The user has claimed that they canceled the game and “exported the build”. What’s more, the person behind the Worms leak has confirmed to Insider Gaming that it’s real: a canceled Garfield Platformer “by the guys who made the lasagna party game.” However, they do not know who the leaker is. At the same time, readers should take this information with a grain of salt.

For those unaware, Garfield Lasagna Party is a party game that was released by Microids and Balio Studio in 2022. It features 32 mini-games across various environments like Garfield’s house, the pizzeria, Liz’s veterinary clinic, and more. The games feature activities like throwing snowballs, navigating obstacle courses, slicing pizzas, and more. Up to 4 players can play together and features different modes like Standard, Challenge, and Competition.

In other news, the canceled Last of Us Online title is still spoken about as the best multiplayer game that some Naughty Dog devs have ever played. Additionally, the voice actor for Miles Morales in Insomniac’s Spider-Man video games has provided insight into why the rumored Venom game will not be released. What are your thoughts on the canceled Garfield platformer? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Resident Evil Requiem DLC shadowdropping. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Eggsterr to Retire from Pro Play after MIBR Match
item.content:encoded:
Evan ‘Eggsterr’ Grady has shared on stream that he intends to step away from the competitive Valorant scene after ENVY’s final VCT Americas Stage 1 contest against MIBR.

Eggster makes a preemptive retirement statement on stream

Speaking on his stream, the American player stated, “I’ve been kind of inconspicuous, but I have decided that the statement that I will make is that I have officially retired from competitive play.”

Eggsterr explained the reasoning behind the decision as linked to recent Yoru nerfs, which rendered his one-trick largely unplayable at the highest level. Eggsterr shared, “I’m not going to lie, they got me. I’m not trying to play other agents.”

The Yoru specialist signalled a return to content creation, stating, “People forget that I’m a content creator who went pro; I’m not a pro player. That’s not where my soul lies. My identity does not lie in pro play.

“I’m not going to lie, pretty much all Stage 2* my head has been in the clouds. I will be playing against MIBR. I’m not officially retired yet. I’m going to leave my heart and soul out there. I’m going to give that game everything I’ve got. I’m going to give that game 130%.”

*VCT Americas Stage 1 is the second stage of the year following Kickoff

The statement follows rumors that ENVY was practicing without Eggsterr, followed by the player sharing the famous gif of Jamal Adams being locked out of The Jets facility, leading him to believe he was cut from the team.

Eggsterr’s incredible rise in Valorant bogged down by ENVY struggles

After securing the Radiant Ranked 1 multiple times on stream, the content creator stepped into competitive play in 2024, playing in a team called RANKERS in Valorant Challengers 2024 NA Split 3.

After winning that split, ENVY signed the roster, replacing Christian ‘nightz’ Smith with Ayan ‘ion2x’ Rastogi, creating the team that would go on to earn a VCT berth with their VCT Ascension 2025 victory.

The Valorant world never saw that roster in action at the highest level, with ion2x and canezerra too young to compete. Angelo ‘keznit’ Mori and Daniel ‘Rossy’ Abedrabbo would join the roster for VCT Americas Kickoff, before the sudden benching of ENVY’s best performer, Hunter ‘inspire’ Schline, after the team’s middle-bracket loss to Cloud9.

VCT Champions 2023 winner, Maximilian ‘Demon1’ Mazanov, joined the roster in the wake of the fallout, but it seemed all was not right behind the scenes at ENVY.

To go from bad to worse, ENVY were met with the news that their future prospect Canezerra would be banned for 12-months, following suspected offensive language.

The ban brought about the termination of the 17-year-old’s contract, removing a potential future piece of the roster and a vital part of RANKERS/ENVY’s climb from Tier 2.

While the content creator can be proud of the incredible rise of his roster, propelling him to pro play, the combination of the roster’s issues and his individual decline after the Yoru nerfs, makes for a somewhat unsatisfying ending to what was one of Valorant’s greatest fairytales.

ENVY currently sits with a 1-3 record in VCT Americas Stage 1, with the first-place team, MIBR, left to play.

You can discuss all things Valorant on our Discord server!
For more Valorant news, see Evil Geniuses’ latest moves.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: When Do Nintendo Switch 2 Prices Increase in 2026? All Prices & Changes, Explained
item.content:encoded:
The news gamers have been anticipating, but what nobody wanted to see confirmed, has officially been announced, with prices for the Nintendo Switch 2 due to increase.

Launched last year, the Switch 2 has been a success for Nintendo, with the latest earnings report stating there are close to 20 million consoles sold, but the rising costs and shortages in the market for core components were something the company could not ignore, and price rises have now been announced.

For all the details on when the price rises and by how much, Insider Gaming has everything you need to know.

When Do Nintendo Switch 2 Prices Increase?

Nintendo Switch 2 prices in the United States, Canada, and Europe will be increased from September 1, 2026, as confirmed by Nintendo, while prices in Japan will rise on May 25.

Rumors of a price rise have been circling for a while, particularly amid global chip shortages and rising costs of building the consoles, and Nintendo has followed suit from other console creators, like Sony, in raising the prices of Nintendo Switch 2.

  • More Insider Gaming: Analyst Worries GTA 6 Players Could Face $1,000 Console Prices

However, the changes, announced on May 8, will not come into effect immediately for the majority of regions. Instead, the price rises will occur from September 1, 2026. Japan is the exception for this, with those changes coming into effect later this month.

That means there is still time to secure a Nintendo Switch 2 console before the price rise comes into effect, which will save you a decent chunk of change.

All Nintendo Switch 2 Prices & Changes

Price increases on the Nintendo Switch 2 console vary around the world, based on different currencies, though the base rate averages out at $50.

The prices of the Switch 2 console in Canada and Europe equal out to around the same $499.99 mark that the United States will now have to fork out, though the Japanese price remains considerably less at under 200 dollars.

  • More Insider Gaming: Switch 2 is Approaching 20 Million Units Sold

Japan’s model is different to the global model, however, offering Japanese language only, and is priced at a local affordability rate, with the language restrictions preventing resellers from exporting the console.

The table below shows all the regions with confirmed price rises for the Nintendo Switch 2, including the exact difference compared to the former price.

What do you think about the price increases for Nintendo Switch 2? Were they inevitable? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Nintendo news, take a look at the announcement of Star Fox, and read about how the Switch 2 version of 007 First Light could be delayed again.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Resident Evil Requiem DLC Shadow Dropped, And It’s Free
item.content:encoded:
Capcom has been dropping hints about forthcoming Resident Evil Requiem DLC for a while. In the blink of an eye, the developer shadow dropped the first DLC for the game, and it’s free for Requiem owners.

Recently, the Resident Evil Requiem devs gave a first update on story DLC, albeit a minor one. If you’re growing impatient, then you can play the new DLC to tide you over until then.

Resident Evil Requiem’s Leon Must Die DLC is Out Now

Resident Evil Requiem’s producer was teasing that a mini-game arrival was imminent.

Which is why anyone snooping on X for clues recently might have been happy to see a new message pop up on the official Resident Evil page. It included a 35-second trailer for the new ‘Leon Must Die’ DLC, so there’s no waiting around.

Check out Capcom’s bio for the update:

‘Leon Must Die Forever is out now as a free update to Resident Evil Requiem! In this new minigame mode unlocked by completing the main story, clear numerous stages while using randomized enhancer abilities as you race against the clock. Will you be able to make it to the end?’

It’s very much a free roguelite/rogulike mode with damage numbers, color-coordinated enemies, a timer, and the Resi 9 experience ramped up to 100!

Check out a snippet of what to expect right here.

Are you already excited to jump into Leon Must Die? Are you hoping for a proper extension of the classic mfenaries mode at some point? Let us know what else you want to see from Resident Evil 9 and story DLC through our Insider Gaming Discord.

If you missed it, check out the fun story of the lewd Resident Evil Requiem Leon merchandise breaking the internet. It’s a crazy world we live in, eh?

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Sony Exploring Generative AI with Bandai Namco
item.content:encoded:
A new partnership has been announced between Sony and Bandai Namco to work on generative AI.

The use of AI and generative AI seems to be growing faster than ever, with many major players exploring this field. Just last month, we saw Ubisoft looking for a generative AI experience in their new hires. Now, it seems Sony has new plans as well, with an announced partnership centered on this tool.

Sony Spoke A Lot about AI in Latest Webcast

Spotted by Destin Legarie on X (formerly Twitter), Sony had a lot to share about AI in the latest call. They first announced a collaborative pilot initiative with Bandai Namco Holdings to explore “how generative AI and the latest technologies can most effectively contribute to delighting the creator’s vision.”

Sony added that repetitive workflows are being automated, software engineering productivity is improving, and new AI tools are accelerating areas such as quality assurance, 3D modeling, and animation. The company spoke about a tool called Mockingbird that can quickly animate a 3D dance model from performance capture.

However, the company states that the role of creators will remain unchanged as AI evolves, as the “vision, the design and the emotional impact of our games will always come from the talent of our studios and performers.” AI will support their work and not replace them.

The goal is to make AI a “consumer-centric experience” that does things like the next game a player might enjoy, along with the next gameplay moment, subscription, accessory, or merchandise that best reflects their passion beyond the store. The company also spoke about PSSR using “machine learning to enhance image quality.”

Sony, Bandai Namco, and Gaudiy’s New Partnership Includes Generative AI Focus

A new announcement from Sony reveals a strategic partnership with Bandai Namco and Gaudiy, with Bandai and Sony investing 10 billion yen in Gaudiy to promote it. Through the new partnership, the companies aim to develop Japanese IP globally and create new experiences and ecosystems that “leverage their technologies and strengths.” There are 5 important themes of the collaboration between Sony, Bandai, and Gaudiy, one of which is Generative AI.

The first goal is to promote and expand Japanese IP. The second goal is to improve IP creation. The third theme is to combine the data assets of each company. The fourth theme is to build a safe ecosystem where fans and creators can participate and operate using blockchain technology.

The final goal of this collaboration is generative AI, with the aim of “create new entertainment experiences by propelling the research, development, and application of Generative AI.” They have already begun working on “concrete initiatives such as using Generative AI image technology within the official GUNPLA (Gundam model kits).”

In other news, the war between physical and digital games continues, as Sony’s FY2025 Q4 stats show a strong preference for digital PlayStation games. Additionally, Sony’s latest earnings report indicates that the PS5 will hit the 100 million mark in sales very soon. What are your thoughts on Sony exploring Gen AI? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the PS6 release date remaining uncertain amid memory prices. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
